title: Opening Keynote, Michael Truell | Compile 26
summary: Cursor announced Cursor Mobile and Origin, an agent-native Git platform, alongside updates to cloud agents and a new internally trained model. The company reported that 95% of usage is now agents, reflecting a shift toward agent-first architecture.
writeup: Michael Truell's opening keynote at Compile 26 covers three Cursor announcements: Cursor Mobile, an Origin Git platform built for agents, and a model trained from scratch. The framing matters because Cursor reports that 95% of usage is now agent-based, and the company is shifting toward an agent-first architecture with dedicated compute resources. The concrete takeaways are the product launches and the stated move away from interactive editing toward agentic workflows backed by purpose-built infrastructure.
